question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user set getBatch().flushInterval to Duration.ofMillis(100) and getBatch().maxItems to Long.MAX_VALUE, creating a scenario where the flush timer fires every 100ms but the batch never reaches maxItems, causing the parser to commit partial batches of CRYPTOTRANSFER items at high frequency, exhausting database connections and causing transaction failures that lose fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring bean aliasing vulnerability to make getStreamType() return StreamType.BLOCK instead of StreamType.RECORD, causing the record file parser to use block-stream metrics tags, corrupting the STREAM_PARSE_DURATION_METRIC_NAME timer and causing the health indicator to always report UNKNOWN, masking a complete CRYPTOTRANSFER parsing outage?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot property source ordering vulnerability to inject a higher-priority property source that sets getBatch().queueCapacity=0, causing the batch queue initialization to fail with an IllegalArgumentException that crashes the importer on startup, preventing any CRYPTOTRANSFER records from ever being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot @ConditionalOnProperty misconfiguration where isEnabled() is evaluated at bean creation time but setEnabled(false) is called at runtime, causing a discrepancy between the bean's active state and the parser's actual behavior, leading to CRYPTOTRANSFER records being silently dropped?
